info.name: 3X-UI Panel API
info.description: Programmatic interface to a 3X-UI panel. Authenticate either by logging in (cookie) or with an API token from Settings → Security → API Token (Bearer). All endpoints under /panel/api/* honour both modes.

item.name: Authenticate with username + password and receive a session cookie. Required before any cookie-based API call.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Clear the session cookie. Requires the CSRF header for browser sessions.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Mint a CSRF token for the current session. The SPA replays it in the X-CSRF-Token header on unsafe requests. Bearer-token callers can skip this — the middleware short-circuits CSRF for authenticated API requests.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Returns whether 2FA is enabled on the panel — used by the login page to decide whether to show the OTP field.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Same shape as /list but with settings.clients[] stripped down to {email, enable, comment} and ClientStats not enriched with UUID/SubId. Use this for list pages; fetch /get/:id when you need the full per-client payload (uuid, password, flow, ...).

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List every inbound owned by the authenticated user, including each inbound’s clientStats traffic counters. settings, streamSettings, and sniffing are returned as nested JSON objects (no escaped strings); legacy callers that send them back as JSON-encoded

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Lightweight picker projection of the authenticated user’s inbounds. Returns only id, remark, protocol, port, and a server-computed tlsFlowCapable flag (true for VLESS / port-fallback on TCP with tls or reality). Use this for dropdowns and attach pickers —

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Fetch a single inbound by numeric ID.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Create a new inbound. Send the full inbound payload (protocol, port, settings, streamSettings, sniffing, remark, expiryTime, total, enable). settings, streamSettings, and sniffing may be sent as nested JSON objects (preferred) or as JSON-encoded strings (

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Delete an inbound by ID. Also removes its associated client stats rows.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Replace an inbound’s configuration. Body shape mirrors /add. Heavy on inbounds with thousands of clients — prefer /setEnable for enable-only flips.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Toggle only the enable flag without serialising the whole settings JSON. Recommended for UI switches on large inbounds.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Zero out upload + download counters for a single inbound. Does not touch per-client counters.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Remove every client attached to a single inbound while keeping the inbound itself. Collects emails from settings.clients[] and feeds them into the optimized bulk-delete path (runtime user removal + traffic-row cleanup + SyncInbound). Destructive and canno

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List the fallback rules attached to a master VLESS/Trojan TCP-TLS inbound. Each rule links one child inbound (the dest) to optional SNI/ALPN/path/xver match criteria.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Replace the entire fallback list for a master inbound. Body is JSON. Triggers an Xray restart.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Reset upload + download counters on every inbound. Destructive — accounting history is lost.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Bulk-import an inbound from a JSON blob (e.g. one exported via the UI). The body uses form encoding with a single "data" field.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Real-time machine snapshot: CPU, memory, swap, disk, network IO, load averages, open connections, Xray state. Cached and refreshed every 2 seconds in the background.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Legacy: aggregated CPU history. Use /history/cpu/:bucket instead — same data with a uniform {t, v} shape.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Aggregated time-series for one metric. Returns an array of {t, v} samples covering the last ~6 hours.

variable.description: cpu | mem | netUp | netDown | online | load1 | load5 | load15.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

variable.description: cpu | mem | netUp | netDown | online | load1 | load5 | load15.

item.name: Xray runtime metrics state — whether the xray config has a `metrics` block, which expvar keys are flowing, and the current snapshot values for each. Returns an empty state when metrics are not configured.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Time-series history for one Xray runtime metric over the last ~6 hours. Same {t, v} shape as /history/:metric/:bucket.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Latest snapshot from the Xray observatory — per-outbound latency, health status, and last-probe time. Only populated when the Xray config has an observatory configured.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Time-series of observatory probe results for one outbound tag. Same {t, v} shape as the other history endpoints.

variable.description: Outbound tag from the observatory config.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

variable.description: Outbound tag from the observatory config.

item.name: List Xray binary versions available for install on this host.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Check whether a newer 3x-ui release is available on GitHub.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return the assembled Xray config that’s currently running on this host.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Stream the SQLite database file as an attachment. Use as a manual backup.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Generate a fresh UUID v4. Convenience helper for client IDs.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Generate a new X25519 keypair for Reality.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Generate a new ML-DSA-65 keypair (post-quantum signature). Returns {privateKey, publicKey, seed}.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Generate a new ML-KEM-768 keypair (post-quantum KEM). Returns {clientKey, serverKey}.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Generate VLESS encryption auth options. Returns an auths array each with id, label, encryption, and decryption fields.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Stop the Xray binary. All proxies go offline immediately.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Reload Xray with the current config. Typically required after structural inbound or routing changes.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Download and install the specified Xray version. Pass "latest" for the newest release.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Self-update the panel to the latest version. The server restarts on success.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Refresh a single Geo file by filename (e.g. geoip.dat, geosite.dat).

variable.description: Filename of the data file to refresh.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

variable.description: Filename of the data file to refresh.

item.name: Refresh the default GeoIP / GeoSite data files. Body can include a fileName, or use the /:fileName variant.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return the last N lines of the panel’s own log.

response.name: Successful response

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return the last N lines of the Xray process log.

response.name: Successful response

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Restore the panel DB from an uploaded SQLite file (multipart form, field name "db"). The panel restarts after restore. Destructive.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Generate a new ECH (Encrypted Client Hello) keypair and config list for the given SNI.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Filter, sort, and paginate clients on the server. Each item is a slim row (no uuid/password/auth/flow/security/reverse/tgId) so the clients page can ship 25-ish rows in a few KB instead of the full table. The response also includes a summary computed acro

query.description: Rows per page. Defaults to 25, capped at 200.
Case-insensitive substring match on email / subId / comment.
Status bucket: online | active | deactive | depleted | expiring.
Match clients attached to at least one inbound of this protocol (vless, vmess, trojan, shadowsocks, ...).
Sort key: enable | email | inboundIds | traffic | remaining | expiryTime.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description: Rows per page. Defaults to 25, capped at 200.
Case-insensitive substring match on email / subId / comment.
Status bucket: online | active | deactive | depleted | expiring.
Match clients attached to at least one inbound of this protocol (vless, vmess, trojan, shadowsocks, ...).
Sort key: enable | email | inboundIds | traffic | remaining | expiryTime.

item.name: List every client with its attached inbound IDs and traffic record. The reverse field, if set, is returned as a nested JSON object (legacy JSON-encoded-string form is still accepted on write).

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Fetch one client by email, including the inbound IDs it is attached to.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Create a new client and attach it to one or more inbounds in a single call. Body is JSON. Per-protocol secrets (UUID for VLESS/VMess, password for Trojan/Shadowsocks, auth for Hysteria) are generated server-side when omitted, so callers can send only the

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Update an existing client by email. Changes propagate to every attached inbound. Body is the JSON client payload — supply the full set of fields you want to keep (the server replaces the row, it does not patch).

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Delete a client by email. Removes it from every attached inbound and drops its traffic record unless keepTraffic=1 is passed.

query.description: Pass 1 to retain the xray_client_traffic row after deletion.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

query.description: Pass 1 to retain the xray_client_traffic row after deletion.

item.name: Attach an existing client to one or more additional inbounds. Body is JSON.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Detach a client from one or more inbounds without deleting the client.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Reset the up/down counters for every client globally. Quotas and expiry are not affected. Triggers an Xray restart if any counter actually moved.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Delete every client whose traffic quota is exhausted (used >= total, when reset is disabled) or whose expiry has passed. Returns the deleted count and triggers an Xray restart when any client was on a running inbound.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Shift expiry and/or traffic quota for many clients in one call. addDays/addBytes may be negative. Clients with unlimited expiry (expiryTime=0) or unlimited traffic (totalGB=0) are skipped for the corresponding field — bulk extend never converts unlimited

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Delete many clients in one call. The server processes the list sequentially so each delete sees the committed state of the previous one — avoids the race the per-email fan-out had on the panel side. Pass keepTraffic=true to retain the xray_client_traffic

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Create many clients in one call. Body is a JSON array of {client, inboundIds} payloads — the same shape /add accepts. Items are processed sequentially; per-email skip reasons are returned for items that fail (e.g., duplicate email). Triggers a single Xray

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Add many clients to a group in one call. Updates clients.group_name and patches the matching client entry inside every owning inbound's settings JSON in a single transaction. If the group name does not yet exist (in client_groups or as a derived label), i

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Clear the group label on many clients in one call. Inverse of /groups/bulkAdd. Clients themselves are kept — only the group label is cleared from clients.group_name and from each owning inbound's settings JSON. Groups become empty if all their members are

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return just the email list of clients that currently belong to the given group. Useful for fanning a single bulk action over an entire group without round-tripping the full client list.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Create a new empty (placeholder) group. The group becomes selectable in client forms and the filter drawer even before any client is added to it. Errors if a group with the same name already exists.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Rename a group. The new name is applied to the client_groups row AND propagated to every matching client (both clients.group_name and the client entry inside every owning inbound's settings JSON) in a single transaction. Returns the number of clients whos

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Remove a group. Deletes the client_groups row and clears the group label from every matching client (both clients.group_name and the inbound settings JSON). The clients themselves are NOT deleted — use /bulkDel after filtering by group for that. Returns t

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List all client groups with their member counts. Merges persisted groups (rows in client_groups, including empty placeholders) with the distinct group_name values currently set on clients. Sorted alphabetically (case-insensitive).

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Attach many existing clients to many inbounds in one call. Each client keeps its identity (email/UUID/password/subId) and a shared traffic row; all clients are added to a target inbound in a single AddInboundClient call. Clients already present on a targe

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Mirror of bulkAttach: detach many existing clients from many inbounds in one call. For each email, intersects the client's current inbounds with the requested set and detaches from those only; (email, inbound) pairs where the client is not currently attac

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Zero up/down counters for many clients in one call. Loops the single-reset path so each client is re-enabled across its attached inbounds and pushed to Xray/remote nodes. Returns the count of successfully reset clients.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Zero out a single client’s up/down counters. Re-enables the client across every attached inbound and pushes the change to Xray (or the remote node) so depleted users can connect again immediately.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Manually adjust a client’s upload + download counters. Useful for migrations from external accounting systems.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List source IPs that have connected with the given client’s credentials. Returns an array of "ip (timestamp)" strings.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Reset the recorded IP list for a client.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List the emails of currently connected clients (last seen within the heartbeat window).

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Map of client email → last-seen unix timestamp.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Traffic counters for a client identified by email.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return every protocol URL (vless://, vmess://, trojan://, ss://, hysteria://, hy2://) for clients matching the subscription ID. Same result set as /sub/<subId>, but as a JSON array — no base64. When an inbound has streamSettings.externalProxy set, one URL

variable.description: Subscription ID, taken from the client's subId field.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

variable.description: Subscription ID, taken from the client's subId field.

item.name: Return every URL for one client across all attached inbounds — the same strings the Copy URL button copies in the panel UI. Supported protocols: vmess, vless, trojan, shadowsocks, hysteria. If streamSettings.externalProxy is set, returns one URL per exter

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List every configured node with its connection details, health, and last heartbeat patch.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Fetch a single node by ID.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Register a new remote node. Provide its URL, apiToken, and optional remark / allowPrivateAddress flag.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Replace a node’s connection details. Same body shape as /add.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Delete a node. Inbounds bound to it are not auto-migrated.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Pause or resume traffic sync with this node.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Probe a node without saving it. Uses the body as connection details and returns the same heartbeat snapshot a registered node would have.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Probe an existing node, updating its cached health state.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Aggregated metric history for a node — same shape as /server/history, scoped to one node.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List configured custom geo sources with their type, alias, URL, status, and last-download timestamp.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List geo aliases currently usable in routing rules — both built-in defaults and the user-configured ones.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Register a custom geo source. Alias is auto-normalised; URL must point to a .dat / .json blob.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Replace a custom geo source. Same body shape as /add.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Remove a custom geo source and its cached file.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Re-download one custom geo source on demand.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Re-download every configured custom geo source. Errors are reported per-source in the response.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Send a fresh DB backup to every Telegram chat configured as an admin recipient. No body, no params.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return every panel setting: web server, Telegram bot, subscription, security, LDAP. The full JSON blob that the Settings page edits.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return the computed default settings based on the request host. Useful to preview what a fresh install would use.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Persist every setting at once. The body mirrors the shape returned by /all. Invalid values (bad ports, missing cert pairs, etc.) are rejected before write.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Change the panel admin username and password. Requires the current credentials for verification. The session is refreshed with the new values on success.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Restart the entire 3x-ui process after a 3-second grace period. The connection drops immediately; the panel comes back online ~5-10 seconds later.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return the built-in default Xray JSON config template that ships with this panel version.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Mint a new API token. Name must be unique and 1-64 characters; the token string is server-generated.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Error response

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Permanently delete a token. Any caller using it stops authenticating immediately.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Toggle a token enabled/disabled without deleting it. Disabled tokens are rejected by checkAPIAuth on the next request.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: List every API token, enabled or not.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return the built-in default Xray config shipped with the panel (identical to /panel/setting/getDefaultJsonConfig).

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return traffic statistics for every outbound. Each outbound shows up/down/total counters.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return the most recent Xray process stdout/stderr output. Useful to check for startup errors or runtime warnings.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Save the Xray JSON config template and optionally the outbound test URL. Both are sent as form fields.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Manage Cloudflare Warp integration. The action parameter selects the operation.

variable.description: data — return Warp stats (quota, remaining). del — delete Warp data. config — return current Warp config. reg — register a new Warp endpoint (sends privateKey, publicKey). license — set a Warp+ license key (sends license).

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

variable.description: data — return Warp stats (quota, remaining). del — delete Warp data. config — return current Warp config. reg — register a new Warp endpoint (sends privateKey, publicKey). license — set a Warp+ license key (sends license).

item.name: Manage NordVPN integration. The action parameter selects the operation.

variable.description: countries — list available countries. servers — list servers in a country (sends countryId). reg — get NordVPN credentials (sends token). setKey — store NordVPN API key (sends key). data — return current NordVPN connection data. del — delete NordVPN data.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

variable.description: countries — list available countries. servers — list servers in a country (sends countryId). reg — get NordVPN credentials (sends token). setKey — store NordVPN API key (sends key). data — return current NordVPN connection data. del — delete NordVPN data.

item.name: Reset traffic counters for a specific outbound by tag.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Test an outbound configuration. Sends the outbound JSON (required), optionally all outbounds (to resolve sockopt.dialerProxy dependencies), and a mode flag.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return the Xray config template (JSON string), available inbound tags, client reverse tags, and the configured outbound test URL in one response.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return base64-encoded subscription links for all enabled clients matching the subscription ID. When the request has an Accept: text/html header or ?html=1, renders a styled info page instead. Default path: /sub/:subid.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return subscription as a JSON array of proxy configs (one per enabled client). Only when JSON subscription is enabled in settings. Default path: /json/:subid.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Return subscription as a Clash/Mihomo-compatible YAML config. Only when Clash subscription is enabled in settings. Default path: /clash/:subid.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Upgrade an HTTP connection to a WebSocket. Requires an authenticated session cookie (Bearer token auth is not supported here). Returns 101 Switching Protocols on success. The server then pushes JSON messages described below.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
